articles.title: Protein repair defects linked to hidden heart failure
articles.description: Researchers in the del Monte Lab at the Medical University of South Carolina (MUSC) are the first to observe defects in the protein repair system associated with the peculiar, misfolded protein plaques previously observed in patients with idiopathic dilated cardiomyopathy (IDCM ).

articles.title: Study explains why walking slows and tires with age
articles.description: Reasons why our walking becomes slower and more tiring with age have been uncovered by new Australian research - with findings showing the body increasingly sacrifices efficiency to stay upright.

articles.title: China’s North Korea outreach 'strategic maintenance', not breakthrough: Analyst
articles.description: Han Lin, Managing Director and China Country Director at The Asia Group, says Beijing’s engagement with North Korea is less about achieving a breakthrough and more about maintaining strategic stability. While Pyongyang has strengthened ties with Russia in recent years, Lin notes that China remains its most important partner and expects greater predictability in return for continued economic engagement.

articles.title: U.S. Bank Asset Management on why they upgraded their 2026 S&P 500 target
articles.description: Rob Haworth, senior investment strategist at U.S. Bank Asset Management Group, says reasonable price-to-earnings multiples, strong AI-led economic activity, and a highly resilient consumer gives him the confidence to upgrade his S&P 500 forecast to 8040 by the end of the year. He also says the broadening of market performance makes it an opportune time to add to positions in industrials and utilities.

articles.title: Study links menstrual cycle timing to COVID vaccine side effects
articles.description: A study of 1,474 Clue app users found that women vaccinated against COVID-19 during the follicular phase had higher odds of self-reported side effects than those vaccinated during the luteal phase.

articles.title: Safety concerns over doctors prescribing experimental weight loss drug not approved by FDA
articles.description: There's a new safety concern about doctors prescribing one experimental weight loss treatment, retatrutide, that hasn't even been FDA approved yet. Adam Yamaguchi reports.

articles.title: Productivity gains from AI are still a couple of years away: Oxford Economics
articles.description: Ryan Sweet, chief global economist at Oxford Economics, says the U.S. "lucked out" as the timing of the energy price spike coincided with the tax refund season, offsetting higher prices at the pump. He also says AI will remain an inflationary force in the U.S. for the next year, but turn disinflationary in 2028 or 2029 as productivity gains become more visible.

articles.title: Eli Lilly CEO: Obesity-care should be healthcare
articles.description: At the American Diabetes Association’s 2026 Scientific Sessions, CNBC’s Angelica Peebles sat down with Lilly CEO Dave Ricks to discuss what his expectations are for insurance coverage of GLP-1s and Medicare’s launch of coverage in July. He believes “we need to reset what we expect from insurance, obesity-care should be healthcare.”

articles.description: MANHASSET, N.Y.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--A critically ill patient at Northwell’s North Shore University Hospital (NSUH) has been one of the first in the United States – and the first on the East Coast – to successfully be treated with an innovative therapy that helps wean those who need mechanical ventilation to breathe on their own faster. articles.description: A University of Minnesota Medical School research team was awarded a 5-year, $3.8 million grant from the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases to study early immune responses in the lung and how they influence tuberculosis infection outcomes.

Russell frames the lab as keeping platform consumability central while accelerating customer-specific AI integration.
